definitions: To pass the critical point in an illness or a difficult situation and begin to improve.
To reach a point where a situation starts to get better after a period of difficulty.
examples: After weeks of being critically ill, she finally turned the corner.
The company's finances were in trouble, but they seem to have turned the corner.
We're hoping the economy will turn the corner soon.
The patient's condition worsened before it finally turned the corner.
